description: When per-agent rate limit is exceeded, the registry MUST return rate_limited (HTTP 429) with the standard error envelope (RFC-ACDP-0007 §4). A Retry-After header SHOULD be present when the retry window is bounded (RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.3 / RFC-ACDP-0007 §6). This fixture pins the wire shape; black-box conformance testing cannot deterministically trigger a rate limit on an arbitrary registry, so this fixture is informative for cross-impl wire compatibility — implementers MUST self-test by submitting publishes faster than their advertised limit.

input.scenario: Producer submits POST /contexts at a rate exceeding the registry's per-agent limit. The exact rate, window, and bucket are registry-defined.
input.body_summary: Any valid PublishRequest signed by the producer being rate-limited.

headers.Retry-After: SHOULD be present. Either an integer seconds value (e.g. '30') or an HTTP-date (per RFC 7231 §7.1.3). MAY be absent when the registry cannot bound the retry window (e.g. concurrency-based limiter without a clear refill horizon).

error.message: Per-agent rate limit exceeded. Retry after the time indicated by the Retry-After header.

response_body_constraints.error_details_optional: details fields (agent_id, scope, retry_after_seconds, etc.) are illustrative and registry-specific; the schema (acdp-error.schema.json) leaves error.details open (additionalProperties: true). Consumers MUST tolerate any subset.

registry_must_not: Return 503 Service Unavailable on per-agent rate limiting (that conflates server-wide unavailability with per-principal limiting; consumers retry differently).
Return a non-application/acdp+json body (RFC-ACDP-0007 §4 mandates the envelope on every failure response).
Include any other registered error code (no overloading of existing codes for rate-limit semantics).
expected.consumer_behavior: On HTTP 429, consumers MUST back off; if Retry-After is present and parseable, consumers SHOULD wait at least that long before retrying. Retry without backoff is a producer bug and MAY trigger registry-level escalation (longer windows, broader buckets).
expected.rationale: Rate-limit responses are a load-shedding signal, not an error in the producer's request content. Honoring the wire shape and Retry-After lets producers behave well under load; ignoring them either fails their work (no retry) or amplifies the load (retry-storm).

implementer_self_test_recipe: 1. Configure the registry with a known per-agent rate (e.g., 10 publishes per 60 seconds for a test agent).
2. Submit 11 valid publishes from the test agent within a 60-second window.
3. Assert the 11th response is HTTP 429 with error.code = 'rate_limited'.
4. Assert Retry-After is present and that a publish submitted after the indicated wait succeeds.
